title: China-to-Nigeria EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: Nigeria does not have a verified official universal public EV connector mandate in the sources reviewed, so connector requirements should be taken from the SONCAP route, applicable NIS or approved IEC equivalent, project specification, vehicle fleet, and network operator requirements. Where IEC connectors are specified, IEC 62196 Type 2 for AC and IEC 62196-3 / CCS-type DC configurations may require hardware changes from a China GB/T product.
target_standards: IEC 62196 series where specified or accepted
Applicable NIS or approved equivalent standard
Project, fleet, and charging-network connector specification
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 20234 for EV conductive-charging connection devices. GB/T 20234 connector geometry, locking, signaling, and communication assumptions should not be treated as drop-in IEC 62196 Type 2 or CCS2 evidence.
rows.gap: Exporters should confirm whether the Nigerian buyer wants GB/T, Type 2, CCS2, CHAdeMO, or another connector ecosystem, then align cable assemblies, sockets, vehicle connectors, pilot / proximity circuits, DC communication, labels, spares, and test reports to that ecosystem.

mandatory_status.target: Project, fleet, network, SONCAP, or accepted-standard dependent; no verified official blanket connector mandate found
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 is a China GB-standard connector baseline and is not automatic IEC 62196 compatibility

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Connector fit is a hardware interoperability question. A GB/T 20234 charger should not be represented as IEC 62196-compatible unless the actual connector set and test evidence prove it.

rows.requirement: EMC Evidence for EV Supply Equipment
rows.target_requirement: SONCAP conformity assessment can include testing and analysis in accredited laboratories, and IEC 61851-1:2017 removed EMC requirements from the general safety text with EMC expected in IEC 61851-21-2 for off-board EV charging systems. Nigeria-specific acceptance should be confirmed through the SONCAP route, applicable NIS, or approved IEC equivalent.
target_standards: IEC 61851-21-2 for off-board EV charging system EMC
Applicable NIS or approved IEC equivalent
rows.china_equivalent: China's common GB-standard EMC baseline for off-board conductive EV supply equipment includes GB/T 18487.2. A China EMC report should be checked for test configuration, port selection, cable length, operating mode, immunity levels, emissions limits, and laboratory accreditation accepted for Nigeria.
rows.gap: Exporters should provide an EMC matrix separating safety-standard clauses from EMC clauses, include test mode details for AC and DC charging, confirm whether communications, metering, payment, or radio modules trigger additional requirements, and ensure lab accreditation is acceptable under the Nigerian route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by SONCAP, applicable NIS / approved IEC equivalent, procurement, radio module approval, or installation approval
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487.2 evidence may support EMC review but does not automatically equal Nigerian accepted EMC evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EMC should be treated as a separate evidence package. A safety report or GB/T baseline alone is not enough if the Nigerian route asks for IEC 61851-21-2, NIS, or accredited-lab EMC proof.

rows.requirement: NERC Grid Connection, Distribution Code, and State-Regulator Coordination
rows.target_requirement: NERC publishes Nigerian electricity-sector codes including the Grid Code and Distribution Code, and its 2026 public notices also identify transition of some intrastate electricity-market regulation to state regulators under the Electricity Act 2023. EV charging sites, especially high-power DC chargers or depots, should therefore be assessed for utility connection, load, protection, metering, safety, and any state-regulator interface.
target_standards: Electricity Act 2023 state-regulator transition where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China EV charger technical files commonly start from GB/T 18487 and GB/T 20234, but a China grid or installation package does not replace Nigerian distributor studies, NERC-code review, metering requirements, site protection design, or state-regulator obligations.
rows.gap: Prepare site load calculations, single-line diagrams, protection studies, earthing drawings, harmonic / power-quality information where relevant, metering architecture, emergency shutdown logic, and distributor / state-regulator approvals for Nigerian installation review.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where grid connection, distribution-network connection, electrical installation approval, or state-regulator approval applies
mandatory_status.cn: GB product conformity does not replace Nigerian site and grid-connection approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EV charger product paperwork is not enough for a Nigerian charging site. Utility and NERC or state-regulator connection requirements should be cleared before installation.

rows.requirement: Emerging EV Policy and Energy Transition Context
rows.target_requirement: Nigeria's Energy Transition Plan states a 2060 carbon-neutrality pathway and identifies transport electrification as part of the emissions-reduction pathway, including high EV uptake in passenger car and public transport segments. This is a policy and market signal, not by itself a product certification rule for imported EV chargers.
target_standards: Project-specific incentives, procurement, or state-level EV requirements where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China's GB-standard EV charger file commonly shows conformity to GB/T 18487 and explicit GB/T 20234 connection-device parts. That evidence may help demonstrate technical maturity but does not establish eligibility for Nigerian incentives, public procurement, local-content preferences, or state EV programmes.
rows.gap: For projects tied to public fleets, bus deployment, local manufacturing, financing, or clean-transport programmes, confirm current incentive and procurement conditions separately from product safety and import conformity.

mandatory_status.target: Generally voluntary or project / incentive dependent unless tied to a specific procurement, financing, tax, or state-level instrument
mandatory_status.cn: GB-standard conformity is technical evidence and not a Nigerian policy incentive determination

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Nigeria's EV policy direction supports market demand, but exporters should not treat the Energy Transition Plan as a charger certification or import-approval instrument.

rows.requirement: IEC 61851 safety baseline where accepted under SONCAP/NIS/approved-equivalent route
rows.target_requirement: SON identifies electrotechnical standardization work and SONCAP accepts applicable NIS or approved equivalents, including IEC standards where accepted in the conformity route. IEC 61851-1:2017 covers EV supply equipment characteristics, the EV-to-EVSE connection, and electrical safety requirements for EV supply equipment up to stated AC and DC voltage limits. The exact Nigerian Industrial Standard adoption record should be verified with SON for the model and use case.
target_standards: Applicable NIS or approved IEC equivalent accepted under SONCAP
SON Electrotechnical Technical Committee standards activity
rows.china_equivalent: China's GB-standard baseline for conductive charging safety is GB/T 18487.1-2023, used with related GB/T 18487 parts and GB/T 20234 connection-device standards. A GB/T 18487 report should be mapped clause-by-clause to IEC 61851 / NIS expectations before it is submitted for Nigeria.
rows.gap: The technical gap is a standards-mapping package: ratings, modes, protective earthing, control pilot behavior, residual-current protection assumptions, markings, installation instructions, environmental ratings, and abnormal-condition tests should be mapped from the Chinese GB file to IEC 61851 / accepted NIS clauses.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory when required by SONCAP route, applicable NIS, procurement specification, or installation approval; exact Nigerian adoption and scope must be confirmed with SON
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 is the China GB-standard safety baseline but does not automatically prove IEC 61851 or NIS conformity

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487 evidence should be treated as a starting point, not a substitute for IEC 61851 / NIS review under the Nigerian conformity route.

rows.target_requirement: SON states that SONCAP is a pre-shipment verification of conformity process for products imported into Nigeria, using applicable Nigerian Industrial Standards or approved equivalents and technical regulations. SON also states that regulated goods subject to SONCAP need Product Certificate and Certificate of Conformity / SONCAP Certificate documents for import and Customs clearance. EV charger scope should therefore be checked by HS code and product description rather than assumed as a blanket whole-unit certification rule.
target_standards: Applicable NIS or approved equivalent standards
Product Certificate, CoC, and SONCAP Certificate for regulated goods
rows.china_equivalent: China's common GB-standard EV charger baseline is GB/T 18487 for conductive charging systems and GB/T 20234 for connection devices. These GB documents can support a technical file but do not replace Nigerian SONCAP route selection, shipment documentation, or acceptance of an applicable NIS or approved equivalent.
rows.gap: Exporters should map the charger, spare parts, cables, and accessories to the import classification, confirm whether SONCAP applies, select the correct Route A, B, or C route if regulated, and prepare test reports, inspection records, shipment documents, labels, manuals, and Nigerian importer records before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for SONCAP-regulated imported goods; whole-unit EV charger scope must be confirmed by classification and SONCAP route
mandatory_status.cn: GB-standard test evidence is useful technical support but is not Nigerian import conformity approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat SONCAP as an import-route and shipment-document question first. Do not claim a blanket Nigerian whole-unit EV charger certificate unless SON or an authorized conformity route confirms it for the exact product.
